findings.description: Table column 'Report Month' data is missing for several rows in the HTML that have no Report Month in the PDF (DV-2013-0005, DV-2013-0006, DV-2013-0007, PC-2012-0036). These are correctly blank in both, so this is not an issue. However, the table header row in the HTML breaks 'As Built Flow MGD' into a multi-line format that could cause misalignment perception, but data appears intact.
findings.evidence: Reviewed all rows - data content matches between PDF and HTML tables.

findings.description: The HTML renders the table in a significantly narrower layout, causing extensive word-wrapping in the Owner-Facility column and Case Number column, making the table much taller and harder to scan compared to the wide single-page table layout in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a wide landscape table fitting on one page. HTML shows a narrow table with heavy word-wrapping, e.g., 'LV-2013-0007' broken across lines, facility names broken into 4-5 lines each.

findings.description: The 'Total Cases: 40' summary row at the bottom of page 2 in the PDF is rendered outside the table in the HTML as plain text rather than as a table row.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Total Cases: 40' appears as the last row of the table. HTML: 'Total Cases: 40' appears as text below the table.

findings.description: The Total Penalties value for case LV-2013-0012 appears as $200.00 in both the PDF and HTML, but this appears to be a source data issue where Penalty Amount ($200.00) + Enforcement Costs ($27.13) should equal $227.13. However, since this matches the PDF, it is not a conversion error.
findings.evidence: PDF and HTML both show $200.00 for Total Penalties on LV-2013-0012. Not a conversion error - retracted.

findings.description: The page header 'DWQ January 2013 Civil Penalty Assessments' and the column headers appear twice in the PDF (once per page) but are consolidated into a single table in the HTML. This is acceptable behavior for page-by-page conversion consolidation.
findings.evidence: PDF has the title and table headers repeated on page 2; HTML consolidates into one table. This is acceptable.
